typecheck.command: echo "No typecheck configured (pure Python, no mypy)"

nonBlockingIssues.description: getJudgmentColors() called twice in watchlist card template — should extract to local variable
Chart width uses one-time window.innerWidth evaluation (acceptable for static dashboard)
Deprecated substr() used in gradient ID generation — should use substring()
innerHTML used with trusted local JSON data for SVG text — acceptable but noted for XSS awareness
SVG text uses alignment-baseline instead of dominant-baseline (minor browser compat)
Dead variable angleRadians computed but never used in donut chart code
Quadrant green/red colors are positionally swapped relative to business semantics (spec ambiguity, not worker error)
Donut stroke-linecap='round' may cause visual overlap on small adjacent segments
Direction chart SVG viewBox hardcoded to 600x300 — could become cramped with 20+ directions
Pipeline-generated data has boilerplate text patterns (identical descriptions, why_it_matters, risks across directions)
All evidence entries have static freshness='最近7天' regardless of actual timestamps
No directions have '值得跟' or '观察中' judgment_state in streetwear data (jade data covers this gap)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update SVG Charts section: (1) Replace 'create elements programmatically via document.createElementNS' with 'template-literal string construction with innerHTML (matching actual codebase pattern)'. (2) Add path arcs as an accepted donut chart technique alongside stroke-dasharray. (3) Clarify that hardcoded hex values are expected/acceptable for judgment state colors in programmatic SVG code.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 5 chart implementations (renderAreaChart, renderProductLineComparisonChart, donut, quadrant shading, evidence stats) use template-literal strings — zero uses of createElementNS found in components.js. Path arcs used for donut. Hardcoded hex used consistently across all chart features.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document bubble matrix axis semantics: X=opportunity (increasing right), Y=risk (increasing up). Document that top-left quadrant = low opportunity/high risk (danger zone, red), bottom-right = high opportunity/low risk (ideal zone, green). This prevents future spec/implementation mismatches on quadrant color assignments.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature spec had contradictory positional vs semantic quadrant labels. Reviewer identified that 'top-left: high opp, low risk (green)' is impossible given the axis layout. Worker followed positional instructions faithfully.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that renderAreaChart() and getJudgmentColors() in components.js are canonical reusable chart utilities. Future visual features should reuse them rather than creating new chart renderers or color mappings.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: renderAreaChart() (components.js:294) used by index.html and direction-detail.html. getJudgmentColors() (components.js:412) used by multiple pages. Both undocumented in AGENTS.md.

rejectedObservations.observation: SKILL.md for dashboard-visual-worker has a typo: '--color-judgment-guancharzhong' should be '--color-judgment-guanchazu'
rejectedObservations.reason: Skill file corrections are outside scrutiny scope — the actual CSS uses the correct variable name per AGENTS.md. Workers reference AGENTS.md, not the skill's pinyin spelling.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker suggested adding cross-page integration verification checklist to bridge-worker skill
rejectedObservations.reason: Skill modification recommendation from a single worker's experience. The bridge-worker skill procedures should be evaluated holistically by the orchestrator, not patched from individual feature feedback.

rejectedObservations.observation: Dashboard/data/frontend_dashboard.json commit policy unclear (should it be committed or git-ignored?)
rejectedObservations.reason: Already implicitly covered by existing convention — jade_dashboard.json is committed, and both serve as default data sources. No change needed.
